cases.semblance: an outward or superficial appearance of something, not necessarily genuine
cases.miscreant: a person who behaves badly or breaks the rules
cases.tantamount: equivalent in effect to something, practically the same as
cases.sacrilege: disrespectful treatment of something considered sacred
cases.proselytize: trying to convert people to your beliefs or religion
cases.ostentatious: showy and designed to impress or attract attention
cases.insinuate: to hint at something indirectly, often something unpleasant
cases.macabre: disturbing and gruesome, involving death or horror
cases.abomination: something that causes disgust or hatred
cases.contort: to twist or bend something into an unnatural shape
cases.cretin: an insulting term for a very stupid person
cases.obliterate: to destroy something completely, wiping it out entirely
cases.decimate: to destroy or kill a very large proportion of something
cases.covetous: having a strong desire for things that belong to someone else
cases.stupor: a dazed state where you're barely conscious or alert
cases.vagrant: a person with no fixed home who wanders around
cases.gremlin: a mischievous small creature blamed for things going wrong
cases.feral: wild and untamed, having reverted from a domesticated state
cases.beanpole: someone extremely tall and thin, often used teasingly
cases.gall: shameless boldness or nerve to do something outrageous
cases.predicament: a difficult or awkward situation with no easy way out
cases.bury one's head in the sand: to deliberately ignore a problem instead of facing it
cases.rumination: the act of turning something over and over in your mind
cases.akimbo: having your hands on your hips with elbows pointing outward
cases.prudish: excessively modest or easily shocked about sex or impropriety
cases.cantankerous: bad-tempered and argumentative, especially in an old person
cases.facsimile: an exact copy or reproduction of something
cases.cairn: a small pile of stones built as a landmark or memorial
cases.desecrate: to violate the sacredness of something, treating it with disrespect
cases.stalwart: loyal, dependable, and sturdy in support of a cause
cases.scion: a descendant or heir of a notable family
cases.wretch: a deeply unfortunate or despicable person
cases.misbegotten: badly conceived, planned, or brought into being
cases.waif: a thin, homeless, or neglected-looking person, often a child
cases.tenebrous: dark and gloomy, full of shadows
cases.inconsequential: not important enough to have any real effect
cases.titanic: of enormous size, strength, or power
cases.ethereal: extremely delicate and light in a way that seems otherworldly
cases.cornucopia: an overflowing abundance of good things
cases.travesty: a distorted, absurd, or grossly inadequate imitation of something
cases.insatiable: impossible to satisfy no matter how much is given
cases.zealot: someone fanatically devoted to a cause or belief
cases.sarcophagus: a stone coffin, especially one used in ancient burial
cases.decorum: polite, dignified behavior that follows social conventions
cases.primordial: existing from the very beginning of time, primeval
cases.sublime: so beautiful or impressive that it inspires awe
cases.machiavellian: cunningly manipulative and willing to use deceit for power
cases.levity: a lighthearted, humorous treatment of a serious situation
cases.preposterous: utterly absurd and contrary to reason
cases.chrysalis: the protective cocoon stage an insect passes through before transforming
cases.miasma: a foul or oppressive atmosphere, often thought unhealthy
cases.harrowing: extremely distressing or disturbing to experience
cases.genteel: polite and refined in a somewhat affected or old-fashioned way
cases.effeminate: having qualities traditionally seen as more typical of women, said of a man
cases.dissuade: to persuade someone not to do something
cases.venerate: to regard someone with deep respect and reverence
cases.seethe: to be filled with intense anger that you're barely containing
cases.menagerie: a varied and often strange collection of animals or things
cases.usurp: to take a position of power illegitimately or by force
cases.odious: extremely unpleasant and deserving of hatred
cases.ephemeral: lasting only a very short time before disappearing
cases.sunder: to violently split or tear something apart
cases.fortuitous: happening by a lucky chance rather than design
cases.charlatan: someone who falsely claims to have skill or knowledge they don't have
cases.penitence: deep regret and sorrow for having done wrong
cases.audacity: bold, shameless nerve to do something considered inappropriate
cases.fratricide: the killing of one's own brother or sibling
cases.disparage: to speak of something in a dismissive, belittling way
cases.befuddle: to confuse someone so much they can't think straight
cases.reticent: reluctant to reveal thoughts or feelings, reserved in speech
cases.pyre: a stack of wood built for ceremonially burning a body
cases.trepidation: a feeling of fear or anxiety about something about to happen
cases.stratagem: a clever plan or scheme meant to outwit an opponent
cases.cauterize: to burn a wound with heat to seal it and stop bleeding
cases.dastardly: wickedly cruel in a cowardly, underhanded way
cases.connive: to secretly plot or cooperate in a dishonest scheme
cases.haughty: arrogantly proud and looking down on other people
cases.cavalier: showing a careless or offhand lack of concern about something serious
cases.parapet: a low protective wall along the edge of a roof, bridge, or balcony
cases.numinous: having a mysterious, awe-inspiring spiritual quality
cases.esoteric: understood by only a small, specialized group of people
cases.ramshackle: rickety and badly in need of repair, falling apart
cases.despotism: rule by a single authority holding absolute, often cruel, power
cases.presumptuous: overstepping what is appropriate by acting with unwarranted boldness
